text:
Assistant: Done — and verified on disk: **`docs/product-spec/capture-redesign.md`** (211 lines, all 12 sections, complete). Everything from this session is now durable before it ages out of context.

What it captures, end to end:
1. **Purpose & mission** — incl. the archaeology mission (explain *why the code looks as it does*) and the regeneration eval
2. **Foundational philosophy** — the 6 invariants (desired-state, recall-bias, distill-don't-dump, Rust-verified citations, integrity-by-construction, two-layer knowledge)
3. **The problem** — both accretion modes + the central reframe (routing & reconciliation as two retrieval-hard problems solved as side effects)
4. **The 4-stage pipeline** — EXTRACT/ROUTE/RECONCILE/HISTORY + the claim-event schema
5. **Authority attribution** — mechanical by evidence-turn, the ratification gate
6. **Authority-asymmetric retention** — keep user "was-A-now-B" archaeology, drop agent noise
7. **The event-sourced destination** — guides as read-only projections, citation stability, conflict flagging, human edits as claims
   - incl. the **projection-is-capture-time-never-inject** constraint
8. **Empirical findings** — the 3-spin experiment, the **routing-is-the-bottleneck** headline, the Spin-1.5 recommendation
9. **Rejected alternatives + why** — prompt-only, action-enum-alone, parallel writes, second log, full-wiki broadcast, pure Spin 2/3 (the "why not," preserved deliberately)
10. **Open questions** · 11. **Roadmap** (Increments 1+2 → destination, `wiki doctor`) · 12. **Model tiering**

I deliberately preserved the **rejected paths and reasoning**, not just conclusions — that "why we didn't do X" is exactly the nuance whose loss re-costs human time, which is the whole problem we're solving. The doc even opens by naming that it's both the deliverable and a live demonstration: one authored coherent spec beats nuance scattered across accreted fragments.
 [...] **(b) Done — spec updated and committed (`9ee72c9`):**
- **§5 rewritten** — the "ratification gate that drops unratified agent claims" is replaced by your **explicit/implicit tag-don't-drop** model, with the lifecycle (promote on user ack, delete on user contradiction) and the OAuth worked example. Includes a dated "Design correction" note explaining *why* the old gate was wrong.
- **§6 aligned** — retention now framed on the explicit/implicit axis (contradicted implicit → delete no breadcrumb; user mind-change → keep archaeology).
- **§8.4 added** — the full archeologist empirical findings (routing confirmed as bottleneck, the precise ROUTE root cause + fix, the three bug-fixes, the coverage finding that *motivated* the §5 correction, and the structural gaps).
- **§11 roadmap** — marked Increment 1 ✅ shipped, Increment 2 ◐ in-progress (now "explicit/implicit tagging" not "ratification gate").

This is the project living its own thesis: refining the spec as understanding evolved — by hand now, which is exactly what we're building it to do automatically.
 [...]    - **docs/product-spec/capture-redesign.md** (authored, 12 sections): philosophy, 4-stage pipeline, §5 authority (explicit/implicit), §6 supersession retention, §7 event-sourced destination, §8 empirical findings + §8.4 archeologist + §8.5 tag-don't-drop result, §9 rejected alternatives, §11 roadmap. Kept updated throughout.
   - Memory files: `inject-gate-disables-reasoning-bails.md`, `inject-compile-synthesizes-with-citations.md`, `supersession-retention-by-authority.md` (later SUPERSEDED), `routing-is-the-capture-bottleneck.md`, `explicit-vs-implicit-direction-tagging.md` (FINAL: metadata-only/keep-all).
